ucs.title: [LLM] Mustang Panda / Stately Taurus PlugX & TONESHELL DLL side-loading via abused signed third-party binaries
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Mustang Panda (a.k.a. TA416 / RedDelta / Earth Preta / Bronze President / Stately Taurus) execution chain where a legitimately signed binary from Adobe, ESET, Avast, Symantec, Norman, or similar is dropped into a user-writable directory and side-loads a malicious DLL adjacent to it (TONEINS / TONESHELL / PUBLOAD / Korplug-PlugX). The combination of ‘legit-signed exe + sibling unsigned DLL + non-ProgramFiles path’ is the actor’s most consistently observed installation pattern across the 2022-2025 SE-Asia / EU government campaigns.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("AdobeUpdate.exe","AcroRd32.exe","AAM Updates Notifier.exe","RoboForm.exe","AvastSvc.exe","AvastBrowserUpdate.exe","ESETSysRescue.exe","eguiProxy.exe","RasTls.exe","BDReinit.exe","BgInfo.exe","Norman Setup.exe","PotPlayerMini.exe","OleView.exe","AutoUpdater.exe","hpqtra08.exe","WerFault.exe") AND (Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\$Recycle.Bin\\*" OR match(Processes.process_path,"^[D-Z]:\\\\")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_id _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process_path as side_load_dir process_id as pid | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t count as dll_drops values(Filesystem.file_name) as side_loaded_dll values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR match(Filesystem.file_path,"^[D-Z]:\\\\")) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_create_time | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rex field=file_path "(?<dll_dir>.*)\\\\[^\\\\]+$" | stats values(side_loaded_dll) as side_loaded_dll values(dll_dir) as dll_dir by dest ] | where like(side_load_dir, dll_dir."%") OR dll_dir==side_load_dir | table firstTime dest user process_name side_load_dir parent side_loaded_dll
ucs.kql:
// Mustang Panda PlugX/TONESHELL side-load chain: legit-signed exe in user-writable dir + sibling DLL load that is NOT signed by Microsoft and NOT in System32
let abused_binaries = dynamic(["adobeupdate.exe","acrord32.exe","aam updates notifier.exe","roboform.exe","avastsvc.exe","avastbrowserupdate.exe","esetsysrescue.exe","eguiproxy.exe","rastls.exe","bdreinit.exe","bginfo.exe","norman setup.exe","potplayermini.exe","oleview.exe","autoupdater.exe","hpqtra08.exe","werfault.exe"]);
let suspicious_paths = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\$Recycle.Bin\"]);
let SideLoadHosts = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where tolower(FileName) in (abused_binaries)
    | where FolderPath has_any (suspicious_paths)
        or FolderPath matches regex @"^[D-Zd-z]:\\"   // removable / non-system drive
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              SignedExe = FileName, SignedExeDir = FolderPath,
              SignedExeSha = SHA256, ExeCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ExePid = ProcessId;
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (suspicious_paths)
    or InitiatingProcessFolderPath matches regex @"^[D-Zd-z]:\\"
| where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath           // DLL sits in same folder as the EXE = side-load shape
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
| join kind=inner SideLoadHosts on $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId and $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.ExePid
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          SignedExe, SignedExeDir, SignedExeSha,
          SideLoadedDll = FileName, SideLoadedDllPath = FolderPath,
          SideLoadedDllSha = SHA256,
          ParentImage, ExeCmd
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Mustang Panda PlugX/TONESHELL side-load on Sentinel via SecurityEvent 4688 + Sysmon 7 (image load)
let abused_binaries = dynamic(["adobeupdate.exe","acrord32.exe","aam updates notifier.exe","roboform.exe","avastsvc.exe","avastbrowserupdate.exe","esetsysrescue.exe","eguiproxy.exe","rastls.exe","bdreinit.exe","bginfo.exe","norman setup.exe","potplayermini.exe","oleview.exe","autoupdater.exe","hpqtra08.exe","werfault.exe"]);
let suspicious_paths = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\$Recycle.Bin\"]);
let SideLoadProcs = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ExeName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ExeName in (abused_binaries)
    | where NewProcessName has_any (suspicious_paths)
        or NewProcessName matches regex @"^[D-Zd-z]:\\"
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, NewProcessId, CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
| extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         Signed = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         Signature = tostring(EventData.Signature),
         LoaderPid = tostring(EventData.ProcessId)
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| where Image has_any (suspicious_paths) or Image matches regex @"^[D-Zd-z]:\\"
| extend ImageDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\"),
         DllDir   = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
| where ImageDir =~ DllDir
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Windows\")
| where Signed == "false" or Signature !has "Microsoft"
| join kind=inner SideLoadProcs on $left.Computer == $right.Computer and $left.Image == $right.NewProcessName
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SignedExe = Image, SideLoadedDll = ImageLoaded, Signed, Signature, CommandLine, ParentProcessName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Mustang Panda's installer chain has used the same template for years across PlugX, TONESHELL, TONEINS, PUBLOAD and Claimloader campaigns: a legitimate signed third-party utility (frequently from Adobe, Avast, ESET, Symantec, RoboForm, BgInfo, hpqtra08, PotPlayer) is unzipped from a RAR/ISO lure into Public, ProgramData, %TEMP% or a USB drive root, and hijacks a sibling unsigned DLL that decrypts t

ucs.title: [LLM] Mustang Panda HIUPAN / MISTCLOAK USB-worm propagation (legit exe + sibling DLL + LNK on removable media)
ucs.description: Hunts the USB self-spreading component used by Mustang Panda since 2022 (HIUPAN.dll, MISTCLOAK, U2EC, the Korplug 'wmiclnt' worm) — the actor drops a triplet onto every connected removable drive: a legitimately signed loader exe, a malicious sibling DLL, and a hidden LNK that masquerades as the drive itself to lure the next victim into double-clicking it. Mustang Panda is one of the very few state actors still actively running USB-worm campaigns against ASEAN diplomatic targets, so this pattern is highly attribution-specific.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_name) as files values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths values(Filesystem.process_name) as writer from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created (match(Filesystem.file_path,"^[D-Zd-z]:\\\\") OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\$Recycle.Bin\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.exe") Filesystem.process_name!="explorer.exe" Filesystem.process_name!="msiexec.exe" Filesystem.process_name!="setup.exe" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | eval has_lnk=if(match(mvjoin(files,"|"),"\.lnk"),1,0), has_dll=if(match(mvjoin(files,"|"),"\.dll"),1,0), has_exe=if(match(mvjoin(files,"|"),"\.exe"),1,0) | where (has_lnk + has_dll + has_exe) >= 2 | where mvcount(files) >= 2 | table firstTime dest user writer files paths
ucs.kql:
// Mustang Panda HIUPAN/MISTCLOAK USB worm: same writer process drops .exe + .dll (+/- .lnk) onto a non-C: drive within 10 minutes
let usb_writes = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FolderPath matches regex @"^[D-Zd-z]:\\"     // any non-system / removable / mapped drive root
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("explorer.exe","msiexec.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","officeclicktorun.exe","onedrive.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe")
    | where FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".lnk"
    | extend ExtCat = case(FileName endswith ".dll", "dll", FileName endswith ".exe", "exe", "lnk");
usb_writes
| summarize
    DroppedFiles = make_set(FileName, 50),
    DroppedPaths = make_set(FolderPath, 50),
    ExtCats      = make_set(ExtCat),
    DropCount    = count(),
    FirstDrop    = min(Timestamp),
    LastDrop     = max(Timestamp),
    WriterCmd    = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
    by DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256,
       bin(Timestamp, 10m)
| where array_length(ExtCats) >= 2                               // at least two of {exe, dll, lnk}
| where set_has_element(ExtCats, "dll") and (set_has_element(ExtCats, "exe") or set_has_element(ExtCats, "lnk"))
| order by FirstDrop desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Mustang Panda USB worm propagation on Sentinel via ASIM ImFileEvent
let usb_writes = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFilePath matches regex @"^[D-Zd-z]:\\"
    | where TargetFilePath !startswith @"C:\\"
    | where ActingProcessName !in~ ("explorer.exe","msiexec.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","officeclicktorun.exe","onedrive.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe")
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".dll" or TargetFileName endswith ".exe" or TargetFileName endswith ".lnk"
    | extend ExtCat = case(TargetFileName endswith ".dll", "dll", TargetFileName endswith ".exe", "exe", "lnk");
usb_writes
| summarize
    DroppedFiles = make_set(TargetFileName, 50),
    DroppedPaths = make_set(TargetFilePath, 50),
    ExtCats      = make_set(ExtCat),
    DropCount    = count(),
    FirstDrop    = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastDrop     = max(TimeGenerated),
    WriterCmd    = any(ActingProcessCommandLine)
    by DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ActingProcessName,
       bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where array_length(ExtCats) >= 2
| where set_has_element(ExtCats, "dll") and (set_has_element(ExtCats, "exe") or set_has_element(ExtCats, "lnk"))
| order by FirstDrop desc
ucs.rationale: USB-borne replication is one of Mustang Panda's most distinguishing tradecraft elements — Trend Micro's Earth Preta reporting, Mandiant's BRONZE PRESIDENT writeups, and Palo Alto's Stately Taurus 2024-2025 ASEAN coverage all describe the same triplet drop (signed loader exe + sibling DLL + hidden LNK that points back at the loader) being written onto every newly mounted drive by a pre-existing inf
